name: WP-TAIL-reseed: re-seed live data into the new identity model
description: Clean-cut tail (after W2 AND W3 AND W4 obligations land) — a single data-migration operation: seed the 8 target tables from the legacy source under the new model; verify row-level integrity before any drop. Must complete before legacy teardown (WP-TAIL-drop-legacy).
acceptanceCriteria: All live entities present in the new model; integrity check passes; reversible until WP-TAIL-drop-legacy runs. Findings: tail-op (no F-ID). Source of truth: _wip/identity-foundation/2026-06-09-phase-o-master-plan.md §1 WP-TAIL-reseed (commit 23d0c01ad)
bodyText:
Bundle brief (transcribed from the frozen Phase-O master plan SS1, WP-TAIL-reseed - source of truth at commit 23d0c01ad):
Bundle rationale: a single data-migration operation; must complete before legacy teardown.
Strategy: seed the 8 target tables from the legacy source under the new model; verify row-level integrity before any drop.
Scope: IN - re-seed live data into the new model. OUT - the drop (WI-586 / WP-TAIL-drop-legacy).
Findings: tail-op (no F-ID).
Acceptance criteria: all live entities present in the new model; integrity check passes; the operation remains reversible until WP-TAIL-drop-legacy runs.
Risk & blast radius: M / risky - live-data migration. Executor protocol hard rule applies: this is a destructive shared-infra step class - the executor must STOP and report the exact planned commands before running anything against a shared database, and wait for shepherd go.
Notes: depends on all of W2 AND W3 AND W4 obligations landing (Cosmo Blocked-by edges: WI-575...WI-584).
